parsed_review.summary: CP00-866 (RP28 Marketplace failure-recovery evidence-review bridge pack, risk C, 150 units, RP28.P07.M06.S03-RP28.P08.M02.S14) is internally consistent and its descriptor-only/no-runtime/no-real-data/no-write boundaries are enforced in code and tests. Pack binding (registry.js:411), deliverable counts (registry.js:1675) and micro-title counts (registry.js:1698) match the baseline/prompt distributions exactly and both sum to 150; the coverage validator (validators.js:2626) checks these against the live plan and the upstream chain CP00-845..CP00-866 is intact with CP866 binding upstream to CP00-865. createRow (registry.js:1909) hard-codes descriptor_only:true and every runtime/install/write/leak flag to false, and the descriptor validator (validators.js:2662) fails closed on any runtime execution, marketplace opening, permission/audit/product-state write, or real-data/secret/token/prompt inclusion. Authority boundary is correct: descriptor (registry.js:3537) and Claude packet (registry.js:2711) keep Claude read-only and non-final-approver, require human approval for runtime opening, and disclaim enterprise trust for local validation; Hermes packet (registry.js:2446) is control-plane only (emits_runtime_receipt:false, writes_product_state:false, no_real_data:true), consistent with hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false. Tests (model.test.js:830-866) assert empty coverage/descriptor errors, runtime-closed rows, and a current CP00-866 contract projection; index.js wildcard re-exports all symbols the validation script imports. Review-hardening checks pass: auth preflight succeeded (exit 0, 1436-byte stdout, not 0-byte/malformed), and the pending-state evidence (command-evidence.json, construction-inspection.json, adjudication.md) is mutually consistent and does not over-claim production_ready or runtime/enterprise trust. Provenance: recorded validation exit codes (rp28:marketplace:validate=0, marketplace node:test=0) are taken from command-evidence.json and were not re-executed during this read-only review; the full pre-closeout validation suite (closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) remains the deterministic closeout gate for the implementer to run. No P0/P1/P2 defects found; one P3 advisory on changed-file-scope bookkeeping. Verdict PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; does not block pack or this CP's closeout goal.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-lists two untracked paths as both pack-implementation and unrelated
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-866/ appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 152-155) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 157-171). These two categories are semantically exclusive, so the overlap is a contradictory categorization. Impact is cosmetic only: active_pack_implementation_refs (lines 5-15) is the authoritative in-pack list and is clean (marketplace src/contract/test files plus the validation script), and no unrelated dirty file (progress-control-room, workbook, docx, ldip invalid-attempt) is pulled into pack scope. The production_ready/closeout decision is unaffected.
findings.recommendation: Adjust the changed-file-scope generator so a path is emitted into exactly one of untracked_implementation_files or unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (the pack's own evidence dir docs/closeout-packs/cp00-866/ should be implementation-scoped, not unrelated). No pack content change required.
